statement: Forced-choice inflation and framework sensitivity are real enough risks that they should be front-loaded as explicit v3 ablations before full-scale sweeps.
rationale: Both v2 and paper mark these as central but under-validated; proceeding without them weakens interpretability.
keyAssumptions: Subset verdict mode can be elicited reliably in the current pipeline.
Rubric swap effects are measurable at pilot scale.
validationCriteria: Run abstain-enabled vs forced-choice condition and compare geometry shifts.
Run rubric-swap condition and measure divergence and certainty changes.
critiquePoints: Prior subset-verdict signal was weak due to abstention dominance.
Rubric mechanism contribution is still hypothetical.
questions: What minimum effect size should trigger expanding this ablation family?
